channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: UK sees lessons from Northern Ireland that could aid the fragile Gaza ceasefire process
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Israelis and Palestinians wait anxiously to see what comes after a ceasefire in the two-year war in Gaza, the experience of the Northern Ireland peace process in the 1990s may offer lessons in the thorny process of moving from seemingly intractable conflict to enduring peace.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Recall: Walmart warns dressers could fall over
item.description: Walmart has issued a recall for a product that the United States Consumer Product Safety Commission says poses a "risk of serious injury or death.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Alaska storm damage so bad many evacuees won’t go home for at least 18 months, governor says
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Damage to remote Alaska villages hammered by flooding last weekend is so extreme that many of the more than 2,000 people displaced won’t be able to return to their homes for at least 18 months, Gov.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN and GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he has commuted sentence of former US Rep. George Santos in federal fraud case
item.description: President Donald Trump said Friday he had commuted the sentence of former U.S. Rep. George Santos, who is serving more than seven years in federal prison after pleading guilty to fraud and identity theft charges.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Man sought to 'evade prosecution through deportation' with surrender: Prosecutors
item.description: The man was facing methamphetamine distribution charges and had indicated he'd plead guilty once he took a trip to see his grandmother.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Andrew gives up his Duke of York title as Epstein allegations refuse to fade
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Andrew said Friday he is giving up his royal title of the Duke of York and other honors after his friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein returned to the headlines.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS and PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: News stories show that reporters may have left the Pentagon, but they haven't stopped working
item.description: Two days after dozens of journalists left their desks at the Pentagon behind rather than agree to government-imposed rules on how they report about the U.S. military, it's apparent they haven't stopped working. Reporters have relied on sources to break and add nuance to stories about U.S.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Legally blind tattoo artist shares journey
item.description: "At first, it was kind of like something that I felt like I had to warn people about,"Morrone said. Now, it's just really become part of my brand and just part of who I am, and I've embraced it.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Cloutier, NewsNation

item.title: Philadelphia officials seek tips about Kada Scott, who disappeared 2 weeks ago
item.description: Authorities in Philadelphia say they are no closer to locating a missing 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job nearly two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 78-year-old man missing in Oregon may have fallen in hole; police ask for help
item.description: In a phone call with his family, Thomas Weightman said he had fallen into a hole and did not know where he was.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, Fox 5

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court to allow deployment of National Guard to Chicago
item.description: The Trump administration has asked the Supreme Court to let it deploy the National Guard to the Chicago area amid President Trump’s push to send military troops into Democratic-led cities.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Indiana University fires student newspaper adviser who refused to block news stories
item.description: Tension between Indiana University and its student newspaper flared this week with the elimination of the outlet's print editions and the firing of a faculty adviser, who refused an order to keep news stories out of a homecoming edition.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FEMA staff sound the alarm on disaster preparedness at rally in front of agency headquarters
item.description: Current and former staff of the Federal Emergency Management Agency demonstrated against workforce and program cuts during a “FEMA Solidarity Rally” on Friday, a potentially risky act of protest because some of the same staffers were placed on leave after signing a public dissent letter in August.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: From bills to berets: Notable moments from NYC's contentious mayoral debate
item.description: New York City’s mayoral candidates have duked it out in their first general election debate, with contenders Zohran Mamdani, Andrew Cuomo and Curtis Sliwa sparring over everything from the Israel-Hamas war to the city’s education system.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The other Alaska airlift: Volunteers save dogs from a flooded Alaska village, 1 tiny plane at a time
item.description: A nonprofit in rural Alaska is working to rescue dogs from Kipnuk, a village hit hard by Typhoon Halong.
item.dc:creator: Cedar Attanasio, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What is the 'AI homeless man prank'? Police says it's dangerous
item.description: An AI-driven TikTok trend is resulting in 911 calls by panicked people who think a man has broken into their homes.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Daniel Cameron banking on his name ID to overcome lackluster fundraising in Kentucky Senate race
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Making his pitch to connect with a Republican crowd that instantly recognized him, Kentucky Senate candidate Daniel Cameron likened the gathering to a family reunion — a feel-good reminder of his status as a party stalwart in the Bluegrass State.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sex is a big market for the AI industry. ChatGPT won't be the first to try to profit from it
item.description: ChatGPT will be able to have kinkier conversations after OpenAI CEO Sam Altman announced the artificial intelligence company will soon allow its chatbot to engage in “erotica for verified adults." OpenAI won't be the first to try to profit from sexualized AI.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: UFO? Military operation? Questions surround balloon seen above Colorado mountains
item.description: Colorado is no stranger to strange objects floating or flying across the sky (remember "Balloon Boy" from October 2009?), and many residents wanted answers for a strange balloon seen floating through the state on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KRON4

item.title: Dressers sold on Amazon recalled due to tip-over hazard
item.description: 76,500 dressers sold on Amazon are being recalled due to the risk of injury or death from a tip-over hazard.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: Many swings, few knockout hits as Cuomo and Mamdani trade jabs in heated first NYC mayoral debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Gov. Andrew Cuomo entered Thursday evening’s first New York mayoral debate trying to blunt Democratic front-runner Zohran Mamdani's momentum.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ace Frehley, Kiss' original lead guitarist and founding member, dies at 74
item.description: Ace Frehley, the original lead guitarist and founding member of the glam rock band Kiss, who captivated audiences with his elaborate galactic makeup and smoking guitar, died Thursday. He was 74.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil can freely travel around US as he fights his deportation case, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge has lifted travel restrictions for Mahmoud Khalil, allowing the Palestinian activist to speak at rallies and other events across the U.S. as he fights his deportation case brought by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Legendary founding KISS guitarist Ace Frehley dies at 74
item.description: (KRON) -- Legendary founding KISS guitarist Ace Frehley has died at the age of 74. He died peacefully surrounded by family in Morristown, New Jersey, according to his agent. Earlier on Thursday TMZ reported he was on life support, and the prognosis was "not good.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Former Trump adviser John Bolton indicted over mishandling classified documents
item.description: A federal grand jury indicted former national security adviser John Bolton on charges of transmitting and retaining national defense information, bringing another criminal case against a critic of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: 9- and 10-year-old charged with attempted murder, rape of 5-year-old
item.description: The Cuyahoga County Prosecutor’s Office announced charges against two juveniles following an assault on a 5-year-old.
item.dc:creator: Talia Naquin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Food assistance is safe through October, but it may be at risk if the shutdown continues
item.description: A federal program that provides food assistance to 40 million low-income people could be at risk in November if the government shutdown isn't resolved by then. And in at least some places, new applications for the program are not being approved.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL and MORGAN LEE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will you see the northern lights Thursday? Auroral show could impact 15 states
item.description: Explosions of plasma and magnetic material are expected to interact with Earth's magnetic field, which means some may soon see the northern lights.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Capital makeover: Trump shows off plans for giant arch in DC
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday detailed plans for an arch to be built near the Lincoln Memorial at the entrance to Washington, D.C., the latest instance of Trump's efforts to give the capital a makeover.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Colleges are fighting to prove their return on investment
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For a generation of young Americans, choosing where to go to college — or whether to go at all — has become a complex calculation of costs and benefits that often revolves around a single question: Is the degree worth its price? Public confidence in higher education has plummeted i
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The world is on track to add 57 superhot days a year, but it could have been worse: Study
item.description: The world is on track to add nearly two months of dangerous superhot days each year by the end of the century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family members share Diane Keaton's cause of death
item.description: Family members of legendary actress Diane Keaton, who passed away Saturday at the age of 79, revealed the Oscar-winning actress died during a battle with pneumonia, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, Fox 5

item.title: Alaska airlifting hundreds from storm-devastated coastal villages
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — One of the most significant airlifts in Alaska history was underway Wednesday to move hundreds of people from coastal villages ravaged by high surf and strong winds from the remnants of Typhoon Halong last weekend, officials said.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER and JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Relatives of Salvadoran family slain in Georgia crash left reeling from tragedy
item.description: TEPETITAN, El Salvador (AP) — Parents, siblings, aunts, nephews and other relatives of a Salvadoran family of eight who died in the fiery crash of a semitrailer and a van in Georgia this week struggled to wrap their minds around the tragedy on Wednesday as they hoped that the bodies of their lost lo
item.dc:creator: MARCOS ALEMÁN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Smucker sues Trader Joe's over alleged 'dupe' Uncrustables product
item.description: The lawsuit also acknowledged Trader Joe’s use of blue packaging is similar to Smucker’s trademarked branding.
item.dc:creator: Rob Taub, WGN 9

item.title: Journalists turn in access badges, exit Pentagon rather than agree to new reporting rules
item.description: Dozens of reporters turned in access badges and exited the Pentagon on Wednesday rather than agree to government-imposed restrictions on their work, pushing journalists who cover the American military further from the seat of its power.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: WGN employee 'violently assaulted' by Border Patrol agents, attorneys say in new statement
item.description: Debbie Brockman, a WGN employee, was detained by Border Patrol agents in Chicago while walking to work, and was released without charges after being held for 7 hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, Fox 5
